“Social Networking Si” submitted by Rojan Aryal and Yunisha Poudel in partial fulfillment  for the degree of Bachelor of Science in Computer Science and Information Technology  (BSc.CSIT),   

SUPERVISOR’S RECOMMENDATION  

It is my pleasure to recommend that a report on “Labor Migration Crisis Management (L-CMC)” has been prepared under my supervision by Rojan Aryal and Yunisha Poudel in partial  fulfillment of the requirement of the degree of Bachelor of Science in Computer Science and  Information Technology (Bsc.CSIT). Their report is satisfactory to process for the future  evaluation.   

…………………………………  

Mr. Hiranya Bastakoti 

Supervisor  

Asian School of Management & technology  

Date: 25th Kartik, 2082  

CERTIFICATE OF APPROVAL  

3

This is to certify that this report has been read and recommended to the Department  of Computer Science and Information Technology for acceptance of report entitled  

ii  

“Social Networking Si” submitted by Rojan Aryal and Yunisha Poudel in partial fulfillment  for the degree of Bachelor of Science in Computer Science and Information Technology  (BSc.CSIT),

ACKNOWLEDGEMENT  

We would like to express our honest gratitude to all those who contributed to this project.  Our sincere thanks go to our supervisor Er. Krishna Kumar Shrestha and our coordinator Mr.  Surya Bam, who provided us with precious guidance and support throughout the project.  Their invaluable expertise, encouragement, and support have been a constant source of  inspiration. We would also like to thank Asian School of management and technology College  for providing us with the resources and support needed to complete this project. Finally, we  would like to extend our gratitude to

4

our families and friends for their support and  encouragement throughout the completion of the project. 

iii  

With respect,  

ROJAN ARYAL  

YUNISHA POUDEL  

ABSTRACT  

The NexusConnect(The Social Networking Site) platform employs a strategic monetization  model primarily based on displaying integrated advertisements to support operational costs  and future development. To cater to user preferences and enhance the premium experience,  the system implements an option for ad removal. Users can choose to permanently disable  all in-platform advertisements by paying a nominal, one-time fee of NPR 100 (Nepali Rupees  one hundred). This transaction is seamlessly processed through the eSewa digital payment  gateway, ensuring a fast and secure micro-payment solution. This feature not only provides a  cleaner, ad-free environment for premium users but also serves as a secondary revenue  stream, demonstrating the project's ability to integrate modern digital payment solutions into  its core functionality.  

Keywords: Social Network, Web Application, User Engagement, Content Sharing, Community  Management, Monetization Model, Ad-Free Premium, eSewa Integration, Micro-Payment,  Admin Dashboard, Real-time Interaction, User Interface.
